stemHtml: The FMS can be used to check the Estimated Position Error (EPE) of the FMS position. What does this value mean to the pilot?

options.a: The EPE is the total position error of the aircraft including the Flight Technical Error.
options.b: The pilot is able to check that the aircraft follows the track as defined in the database.
options.c: The EPE value must be equal to or less than the required navigation specification.
options.d: The pilot uses this value to assess the Flight Technical Error of the aircraft.
